channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about the tensions between Iran and the US before their second round of talks
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran and the United States will hold talks Saturday in Rome, their second round of negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program. The talks follow a first round held in Muscat, Oman, where the two sides spoke face to face.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former President Bill Clinton returns to Oklahoma City 30 years after the bombing
item.description: Thirty years after the deadliest homegrown attack in U.S. history, former President Bill Clinton will return to Oklahoma City for a remembrance ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Ukraine-Russia talks 'coming to a head' and 'no one is playing' him in push to end war
item.description: PARIS (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said negotiations between Ukraine and Russia are “coming to a head” and insisted that neither side is “playing” him in his push to end the grinding war. Trump spoke shortly after Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Iran and the US prepare for a second round of negotiations over Tehran's nuclear program in Rome
item.description: ROME (AP) — Iran and the United States prepared Saturday for a second round of negotiations over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program in Rome The talks in Italy over Easter weekend again will hinge on U.S. billionaire Steve Witkoff, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fired probationary workers at Commerce say their insurance is being cut early
item.description: Probationary workers who were refired this month from the Commerce Department say their health insurance is being terminated earlier than they expected.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to further enforce order restoring AP access to White House
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to further enforce his order directing the White House to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces, signaling that while top officials are owed a presumption of good faith, violations of his directive would be met with a heavy hand. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Nintendo announces new date for Switch 2 pre-orders
item.description: "Mario Kart World" and "Donkey Kong Bananza" will launch alongside the console, with prices holding steady at $79.99 and $69.99, respectively. Digital and physical editions will be offered at the same price point.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster and other allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, Fox 5

item.title: 67% of CEOs surveyed disapprove of Trump's tariffs
item.description: A survey of more than 300 American business owners and CEOs revealed that 76% of the group believe tariffs will hurt their companies.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 10,000 pages of records about Robert F. Kennedy's 1968 assassination are released, on Trump's order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About 10,000 pages of records related to the 1968 assassination of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy were released Friday, including handwritten notes by the gunman, who said the Democratic presidential candidate “must be disposed of” and acknowledged an obsession with killing him.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and HAYA PANJWANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit challenges Trump administration crackdown on international students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A class action lawsuit filed Friday asks a federal court to reinstate the legal status of international students caught up in a Trump administration crackdown that has left more than a thousand fearful of deportation.
item.dc:creator: ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For some, the Florida State University shooting is a grim repeat of the Parkland massacre
item.description: A few of the people who were at Florida State University for a deadly shooting were also traumatized by the Parkland massacre seven years ago.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre And Stephany Matat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FAA investigating after 'green laser' flashed at Black Hawk helicopter
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration reported it is investigating an incident on Thursday involving a laser aimed at a Black Hawk helicopter operated by the National Guard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana reports first measles cases since 1990
item.description: Two children have died amid the outbreak this year and a third death is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana takes a first -- and long -- shot at acquiring parts of Illinois
item.description: Indiana is taking a first step toward acquiring parts of Illinois, though a last step appears rather unlikely.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows doctor with measles treating kids. RFK Jr later praised him as an ‘extraordinary’ healer
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — A Texas doctor who has been treating children in a measles outbreak was shown on video with a measles rash on his face in a clinic a week before Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. met him and praised him as an “extraordinary” healer. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia school shooting suspect's dad fights 'epicenter' murder trial: attorney
item.description: Colin Gray, charged with 29 crimes after his son allegedly killed four people in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School, was granted a venue change for his trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military officials identify 2 Marines killed in crash during border deployment
item.description: SANTA TERESA, N.M. (AP) — Military officials have identified the two Marines from California who were killed when their vehicle crashed as a convoy was traveling along the U.S.-Mexico border near Santa Teresa, New Mexico. They were Lance Cpl. Albert Aguilera, 22, of Riverside and Lance Cpl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A US citizen was held for pickup by ICE even after proving he was born in the country
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A U.S. citizen was arrested in Florida for allegedly being in the country illegally and held for pickup by immigration authorities even after his mother showed a judge her son’s birth certificate and the judge dismissed charges.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trader Joe's is planning to open 21 new locations, including in California
item.description: Is your neighborhood lacking a grocery store that could easily double a well-lit tiki bar? That may change in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Menendez brothers attorney's focus on grisly photos part of strategic defense play to free killers: experts
item.description: Lyle and Erik Menendez will return to court on May 9 in their bid for resentencing, as legal experts break down the defense's strategy to reduce the killer brothers' life sentences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor in Germany accused of 15 murders, suspected in 95 more
item.description: A 40-year-old doctor practicing palliative medicine in Germany has been charged with killing 15 of his patients and is suspected in another 95 cases, NBC News reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to block the university from enrolling international students as the president called for withdrawing Harvard’s tax-exempt status.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, FATIMA HUSSEIN and ALIA WONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: U.S.-born American citizen under ICE hold in Florida after driving from Georgia
item.description: A U.S.-born American citizen was being detained at the request of immigration authorities Thursday despite an advocate showing his U.S. birth certificate in court and a county judge finding no reason for him to be considered an “illegal alien” who illegally entered Florida.
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare killing suspect Luigi Mangione indicted as prosecutors push for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Luigi Mangione was indicted Thursday on a federal murder charge in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, a required step as prosecutors work to make good on the Trump administration’s order to seek the death penalty for what it called a “premeditated, cold-blooded assas
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: Check your Venmo, email and mailbox: A payment from a years-old class action lawsuit against Facebook may be on its way.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the shooting at Florida State University
item.description: Students and staff have returned to buildings on the Florida State University campus to retrieve their belongings a day after a gunman opened fire, killing two people and wounding at least six others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Obama, Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: Former President Barack Obama, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson are among the most liked politicians, according to new YouGov surveys.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family holds funeral for young mother, only to learn she's still alive
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their loved one, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state. Shanita Hopkins reached out to Nexstar's WROC with a very unusual situation.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, KTLA 5

item.title: Over 150 dogs rescued from 'extremely poor' conditions inside home
item.description: 152 dogs were rescued from a home in Ogden, Utah, where they were reportedly living in "extremely poor and overcrowded" conditions, according to the Ogden Police Department. Lt.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KTLA 5
